description_long: This is a medieval-style battle axe featuring a double-edged, dark metallic blade with a pronounced central spike extending horizontally. The blade has a curved, crescent shape with cut-out sections, giving it a menacing and ornate appearance. The handle is long, cylindrical, and appears to be made of wood with a textured, striped pattern, possibly for improved grip. The axe head is securely mounted atop the handle, with the blade's edges extending outward and the spike projecting forward. The overall design suggests it is both a functional tool and a weapon, suitable for display or use. The materials and craftsmanship evoke a rugged, utilitarian aesthetic typical of historical axes.
description: A medieval-style double-edged battle axe with a dark, ornate blade and a long, textured wooden handle. The axe features a central spike and crescent-shaped edges.
description_view_0: The axe is shown from a three-quarter angle, highlighting the double-edged blade, central spike, and the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed directly from the side, showing the thin profile of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: The opposite three-quarter view reveals the other side of the blade, again emphasizing the crescent shape and the spike.
description_view_3: A direct front view displays the narrowest profile of the axe, with the spike and blade edges aligned, and the handle extending downward.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight cylindrical wooden handle with a striped, textured pattern for grip; round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Double-edged, crescent-shaped steel blade with a central horizontal spike and decorative cut-outs; no beard, pronounced poll.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted atop the handle.
